title: rubus contractifolius
intro: Rubus contractifolius, also known as the Australian bramble or prickly rose, is a species of thorny shrub native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Rubus contractifolius include relief from menstrual cramps, arthritis, and skin irritations, as well as support for digestive health. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water or using the roots in tea form, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses.
